question: For the quarterly online platform data, what can be concluded about the trend in the absolute number of online searches over the year from this 100% stacked bar chart?

options.A: The absolute number of online searches increases quarter by quarter, because the share of searches rises in later quarters.
options.B: The chart normalizes unrelated categories to 100% per quarter, so it only shows the share of online searches and cannot reveal the trend in their absolute number.
options.C: The absolute number of online searches remains roughly constant across all quarters, as indicated by the similar bar heights.
options.D: The absolute number of online searches decreases over the year, because complaints take up a larger portion of the bars.

explanation: The chart normalizes unrelated categories to 100% within each quarter. It can suggest that the share of a metric is higher in later quarters, but it cannot establish an absolute increasing trend without the underlying totals. A higher percentage share does not necessarily mean a higher absolute quantity.
